briefing.headline: Rig up a Spinnerbait — aggressive spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Spinnerbait is a strong choice for spawn with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Use a heavier 3/4-ounce spinnerbait in windy conditions — easier to cast and the positive blade resistance makes it easier to feel bites than finesse presentations

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use a heavier 3/4-ounce spinnerbait in windy conditions — easier to cast and the positive blade resistance makes it easier to feel bites than finesse presentations
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, dock, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Trim the skirt and add a narrow shad profile soft plastic trailer to improve visual attraction and hooking percentages; bass take the bait deeper and hold on longer than with a skirt alone
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, dock, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Don't assume you must finesse spawning bass. At the 2011 Classic, 4 of top 7 finishers power fished spinnerbaits and crankbaits on the same 150-yard spawning flat. Key conditions: hard bottom flat with stumps + grass, 10-degree water temp ri...

Wheeler: The 7'1" MH casting rod is great for jigs, spinnerbaits, and single-hook baits around tight cover like docks, laydowns, and overhangs; the 7'4" MH moderate-fast casting rod is great for offshore cranking and big swimbaits with its parabol...
Hackney: 'Nine times out of 10 you'll find better success with a spinnerbait fishing around isolated cover like dock posts, stumps, and boulders. Bass position near cover to ambush prey.'
Johnston: Uses Garmin LiveScope Perspective Mode to spot individual bed fish in rippled conditions when you can't see them visually. Cast to them and catch on first attempt.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal spawning bass are active on beds in 2-5ft. Falling pressure often triggers new waves of fish to move onto beds. Target hard-bottom spawning flats, secondary pockets, and protected coves.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
